TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: told by Giɫgiɫaw of Vaɫulu village

S.FORM: malada nu aya, maretimaɫimaɫi a tsemas niamadju.
S.PHON: maɭada nu aja, marətimaʎimaʎi a ʦəmas niamaɉu.
S.FORM: malada nu aya, maretimaljimalji a cemas niamadju.
S.PHON: maɭada nu aja, marətimaʎimaʎi a ʦəmas niamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: When we speak of shamans, they have various gods.

S.FORM: ɫakua tjara tadjaladjalan a sidjapesan a sikapalisian a pagaɫu.
S.PHON: ʎakua cara taɉaɭaɉaɭan a siɉapəsan a sikapaɭisian a pagaʎu.
S.FORM: ljakua tjara tadjaladjalan a sidjapesan a sikapalisian a pagalju.
S.PHON: ʎakua ʦara taɉaɭaɉaɭan a siɉapəsan a sikapaɭisian a pagaʎu.
S.TRANSL: But they are all more or less the same in their blowing (and) rituals.

S.FORM: aitsu a malada nu izua saqetju djapesen niamadju.
S.PHON: aiʦu a maɭada nu izua saqəcu ɉapəsən niamaɉu.
S.FORM: aicu a malada nu izua saqetju djapesen niamadju.
S.PHON: aiʦu a maɭada nu izua saqəʦu ɉapəsən niamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: When someone is in pain the shamans blow on them.

S.FORM: nu selapay sitjetsemas niamadju a malada, sa djauɫan tua salasaladj niamadju, sana kapalisilisi niamadju.
S.PHON: nu səɭapaj sicəʦəmas niamaɉu a maɭada, sa ɉauʎan tua saɭasaɭaɉ niamaɉu, sana kapaɭisiɭisi niamaɉu.
S.FORM: nu selapay sitjecemas niamadju a malada, sa djauljan tua salasaladj niamadju, sana kapalisilisi niamadju.
S.PHON: nu səɭapaj siʦəʦəmas niamaɉu a maɭada, sa ɉauʎan tua saɭasaɭaɉ niamaɉu, sana kapaɭisiɭisi niamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: If it’s a severe case the shamans go into a trance for them and summon their spirit companions and perform rites.

S.FORM: qau nu palisi tiamadju, venay itjen tua tjinutsauan.
S.PHON: qau nu paɭisi tiamaɉu, vənaj icən tua cinuʦauan.
S.FORM: qau nu palisi tiamadju, venay itjen tua tjinucauan.
S.PHON: qau nu paɭisi tiamaɉu, vənaj iʦən tua ʦinuʦauan.
S.TRANSL: When they perform rites, we give gifts for the spirits.

S.FORM: vaqu a sipatjutsautsau.
S.PHON: vaqu a sipacuʦauʦau.
S.FORM: vaqu a sipatjucaucau.
S.PHON: vaqu a sipaʦuʦauʦau.
S.TRANSL: Millet is the gift for summoning spirits to human beings(?).

S.FORM: nu neka, paisu anga, lami anga a zuma.
S.PHON: nu nəka, paisu aŋa, ɭami aŋa a zuma.
S.FORM: nu neka, paisu anga, lami anga a zuma.
S.PHON: nu nəka, paisu aŋa, ɭami aŋa a zuma.
S.TRANSL: If we haven’t any, then money or some other staple food.

S.FORM: saka izua uta a sinikipapuluqem a sinitjuap a sinikipuvatjuɫayan a tja pinakitjukuda nu venay itjen.
S.PHON: saka izua uta a sinikipapuɭuqəm a sinicuap a sinikipuvacuʎajan a ca pinakicukuda nu vənaj icən.
S.FORM: saka izua uta a sinikipapuluqem a sinitjuap a sinikipuvatjuljayan a tja pinakitjukuda nu venay itjen.
S.PHON: saka izua uta a sinikipapuɭuqəm a siniʦuap a sinikipuvaʦuʎajan a ʦa pinakiʦukuda nu vənaj iʦən.
S.TRANSL: And we also differentiate between gifts for success, for summoning spirits, for charms(?).

S.FORM: nu selapay aravats tja sitsuɫu tua qatsang.
S.PHON: nu səɭapaj aravaʦ ca siʦuʎu tua qaʦaŋ.
S.FORM: nu selapay aravac tja siculju tua qacang.
S.PHON: nu səɭapaj aravaʦ ʦa siʦuʎu tua qaʦaŋ.
S.TRANSL: If it’s a very serious case we kill a pig.

S.FORM: nu tsemuɫu izua za zinatjak aya a sinipatjavang tua tsemas.
S.PHON: nu ʦəmuʎu izua za zinacak aja a sinipacavaŋ tua ʦəmas.
S.FORM: nu cemulju izua za zinatjak aya a sinipatjavang tua cemas.
S.PHON: nu ʦəmuʎu izua za zinaʦak aja a sinipaʦavaŋ tua ʦəmas.
S.TRANSL: When we kill it, part is ‘set aside’ as an offering to the gods (to be eaten only by the immediate family).

S.FORM: azua zuma nu vutjuɫan mana ika makudanga a kanen nua mareka kaka.
S.PHON: azua zuma nu vucuʎan mana ika makudaŋa a kanən nua marəka kaka.
S.FORM: azua zuma nu vutjuljan mana ika makudanga a kanen nua mareka kaka.
S.PHON: azua zuma nu vuʦuʎan mana ika makudaŋa a kanən nua marəka kaka.
S.TRANSL: The rest of the meat can be eaten by the relations.

S.FORM: aitsu a qemazaɫ tjara kitsuɫu sakamaya.
S.PHON: aiʦu a qəmazaʎ cara kiʦuʎu sakamaja.
S.FORM: aicu a qemazalj tjara kiculju sakamaya.
S.PHON: aiʦu a qəmazaʎ ʦara kiʦuʎu sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: When there is a haunting(?) we certainly join in a sacrificial feast.

S.FORM: azua malada nu izua a naminvalak, sualapan tua mareka qatsiɫay tua mareka valiseqaɫ tua mareka qidung.
S.PHON: azua maɭada nu izua a naminvaɭak, suaɭapan tua marəka qaʦiʎaj tua marəka vaɭisəqaʎ tua marəka qiduŋ.
S.FORM: azua malada nu izua a naminvalak, sualapan tua mareka qaciljay tua mareka valiseqalj tua mareka qidung.
S.PHON: azua maɭada nu izua a naminvaɭak, suaɭapan tua marəka qaʦiʎaj tua marəka vaɭisəqaʎ tua marəka qiduŋ.
S.TRANSL: When someone has a swelling the shaman removes it with stones, wood-chips or charcoal.

S.FORM: nu izua piqay, nu naminvalak a tja kinatsavatsavan, malaing anga a nasema varung tjayamadju tua siqaqivu.
S.PHON: nu izua piqaj, nu naminvaɭak a ca kinaʦavaʦavan, maɭaiŋ aŋa a nasəma varuŋ cajamaɉu tua siqaqivu.
S.FORM: nu izua piqay, nu naminvalak a tja kinacavacavan, malaing anga a nasema varung tjayamadju tua siqaqivu.
S.PHON: nu izua piqaj, nu naminvaɭak a ʦa kinaʦavaʦavan, maɭaiŋ aŋa a nasəma varuŋ ʦajamaɉu tua siqaqivu.
S.TRANSL: If we have sores or swellings on our bodies, the shamans say whatever comes to their minds to explain it.

S.FORM: vinuɫuq aya.
S.PHON: vinuʎuq aja.
S.FORM: vinuljuq aya.
S.PHON: vinuʎuq aja.
S.TRANSL: We are told that spirits have thrown something at us,

S.FORM: djinukul aya.
S.PHON: ɉinukuɭ aja.
S.FORM: djinukul aya.
S.PHON: ɉinukuɭ aja.
S.TRANSL: have hit us,

S.FORM: sinizurung sun aya.
S.PHON: sinizuruŋ sun aja.
S.FORM: sinizurung sun aya.
S.PHON: sinizuruŋ sun aja.
S.TRANSL: have pushed us,

S.FORM: nasetsevung sun ayain itjen.
S.PHON: nasəʦəvuŋ sun ajain icən.
S.FORM: nasecevung sun ayain itjen.
S.PHON: nasəʦəvuŋ sun ajain iʦən.
S.TRANSL: or we have met a spirit.

S.FORM: aitsu a malada makaya djemauɫ tua tja tjaisangas sa tja kiqaqivuan.
S.PHON: aiʦu a maɭada makaja ɉəmauʎ tua ca caisaŋas sa ca kiqaqivuan.
S.FORM: aicu a malada makaya djemaulj tua tja tjaisangas sa tja kiqaqivuan.
S.PHON: aiʦu a maɭada makaja ɉəmauʎ tua ʦa ʦaisaŋas sa ʦa kiqaqivuan.
S.TRANSL: Shamans can summon our ancestors so that we can converse with them.

S.FORM: nu izua a tja sikivadavadaq, paruqu itjen tua zaqu.
S.PHON: nu izua a ca sikivadavadaq, paruqu icən tua zaqu.
S.FORM: nu izua a tja sikivadavadaq, paruqu itjen tua zaqu.
S.PHON: nu izua a ʦa sikivadavadaq, paruqu iʦən tua zaqu.
S.TRANSL: If we have a question we put a crystal bead (on a gourd).

S.FORM: nu sitseker, ɫemaui aya.
S.PHON: nu siʦəkər, ʎəmaui aja.
S.FORM: nu siceker, ljemaui aya.
S.PHON: nu siʦəkər, ʎəmaui aja.
S.TRANSL: If it stays on, the answer is Yes.

S.FORM: izua i tjarivavaw a umaq nua tsemas.
S.PHON: izua i carivavaw a umaq nua ʦəmas.
S.FORM: izua i tjarivavaw a umaq nua cemas.
S.PHON: izua i ʦarivavaw a umaq nua ʦəmas.
S.TRANSL: The gods live in heaven.

S.FORM: nu ini anan ka patjeɫuin inika makaya a tjumaq i tjarivavaw.
S.PHON: nu ini anan ka pacəʎuin inika makaja a cumaq i carivavaw.
S.FORM: nu ini anan ka patjeljuin inika makaya a tjumaq i tjarivavaw.
S.PHON: nu ini anan ka paʦəʎuin inika makaja a ʦumaq i ʦarivavaw.
S.TRANSL: If a shaman hasn’t yet qualified she can’t get into heaven.

S.FORM: qau nu tjumaq i tjarivavaw, ventjek azua malada.
S.PHON: qau nu cumaq i carivavaw, vəncək azua maɭada.
S.FORM: qau nu tjumaq i tjarivavaw, ventjek azua malada.
S.PHON: qau nu ʦumaq i ʦarivavaw, vənʦək azua maɭada.
S.TRANSL: When a shaman is going to enter heaven, she performs a rite of breaking bamboo.

S.FORM: nu uri malada tiamadju mesuaw a gemdeged sana pangtjengtjezi, sana sane djaladjalan.
S.PHON: nu uri maɭada tiamaɉu məsuaw a gəmdəgəd sana paŋcəŋcəzi, sana sanə ɉaɭaɉaɭan.
S.FORM: nu uri malada tiamadju mesuaw a gemdeged sana pangtjengtjezi, sana sane djaladjalan.
S.PHON: nu uri maɭada tiamaɉu məsuaw a gəmdəgəd sana paŋʦəŋʦəzi, sana sanə ɉaɭaɉaɭan.
S.TRANSL: When shamans are going to perform a trance rite, they yawn repeatedly and then the spirits come on them, and they make their way (to heaven).

S.FORM: nu qematsuvung seman djalan uta sana patjezua.
S.PHON: nu qəmaʦuvuŋ səman ɉaɭan uta sana pacəzua.
S.FORM: nu qemacuvung seman djalan uta sana patjezua.
S.PHON: nu qəmaʦuvuŋ səman ɉaɭan uta sana paʦəzua.
S.TRANSL: When they have finished they make a way back and that’s the end.

S.FORM: nu makakivaqu pakasalut tua tsemas; nu makakiɫumay uta.
S.PHON: nu makakivaqu pakasaɭut tua ʦəmas; nu makakiʎumaj uta.
S.FORM: nu makakivaqu pakasalut tua cemas; nu makakiljumay uta.
S.PHON: nu makakivaqu pakasaɭut tua ʦəmas; nu makakiʎumaj uta.
S.TRANSL: When we have finished harvesting millet we make a feast for the gods, and also when we have finished harvesting barnyard millet.

S.FORM: azua zuma nu maladan palisi a tjemamaku, palisi a keman tua paday tua masikau.
S.PHON: azua zuma nu maɭadan paɭisi a cəmamaku, paɭisi a kəman tua padaj tua masikau.
S.FORM: azua zuma nu maladan palisi a tjemamaku, palisi a keman tua paday tua masikau.
S.PHON: azua zuma nu maɭadan paɭisi a ʦəmamaku, paɭisi a kəman tua padaj tua masikau.
S.TRANSL: For some shamans smoking is taboo, and also eating rice and wine from the plains(?).

S.FORM: ɫakua inika palisi a zuma.
S.PHON: ʎakua inika paɭisi a zuma.
S.FORM: ljakua inika palisi a zuma.
S.PHON: ʎakua inika paɭisi a zuma.
S.TRANSL: But for some there is no such taboo.

S.FORM: nu sangasangasan vayan tua zaqu a mapuɫat.
S.PHON: nu saŋasaŋasan vajan tua zaqu a mapuʎat.
S.FORM: nu sangasangasan vayan tua zaqu a mapuljat.
S.PHON: nu saŋasaŋasan vajan tua zaqu a mapuʎat.
S.TRANSL: When shamans begin they are all given a crystal bead.

S.FORM: ɫakua neka nu patsutsunan niamadju a tsemas.
S.PHON: ʎakua nəka nu paʦuʦunan niamaɉu a ʦəmas.
S.FORM: ljakua neka nu pacucunan niamadju a cemas.
S.PHON: ʎakua nəka nu paʦuʦunan niamaɉu a ʦəmas.
S.TRANSL: But they don’t really see gods.

S.FORM: ɫakua tiamadju a kinemnemnem tua kakudan niamadju.
S.PHON: ʎakua tiamaɉu a kinəmnəmnəm tua kakudan niamaɉu.
S.FORM: ljakua tiamadju a kinemnemnem tua kakudan niamadju.
S.PHON: ʎakua tiamaɉu a kinəmnəmnəm tua kakudan niamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: It’s they themselves who think up their customs.
